dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Here comes Laurel, or whatever her name is. I was just thinking about the time we met. I looked down into the shimmering waters... and there he was. Our eyes locked; like he could see my soul. We've been together ever since.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have to say, I don't think I've met anyone who's quite so taken with themselves as you, Narcissus. I'm a little envious. When I see myself... I see so many flaws.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oof, I know what you mean. Not everybody's born with perfect skin... lustrous hair... total package. But everybody's beautiful in their own way! Even you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure is nice here, isn't it? The air is thick, the waters, crystal clear. And there's no rain! I couldn't see myself at all when it would rain.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm pleased to know that the untended outer reaches of the Underworld exceed your expectations. Surely Lord Hades would want all his subjects to be well accommodated here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on I know not everybody's gonna get a spot as sweet as this. The best spots in all the Underworld go to those who earned those spots in life, right? Well no wonder!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nothing gets to you! A war rages between the Titan Chronos and the gods, but you've shown not even the slightest concern. What's your secret?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't keep secrets, Laurel. It's just that... I don't care. If it doesn't affect me, it doesn't affect me. Got enough problems of my own... such as, how best to get rid of all these.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Maybe it is a curse... this thing I've got. The way that everybody falls for me the moment they lay eyes on me, I mean I get it! But still. They don't even know me....

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Mere infatuation, in the guise of love... though to your point, your suitors might do well to interrogate their feelings once in a while. And respect your wish for privacy.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well the Shades around here aren't so bad I guess. They know where we stand! Back when I lost myself in these waters... they kept me company. Like you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Don't the vicious creatures contaminating these chambers make this a rather dangerous place in which to laze about?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I've made clear to them that they don't stand a chance with me at all. In my position, it's not good to send mixed messages. Both for my suitor's sakes... and for my own.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're very focused. Centered on the self. Become inured somehow to the incessant singing that always echoes through these halls.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Speak not to me of Echo's this or Echo's that, OK? Echo meant nothing to me and somehow got the wrong idea anyway. And don't you get any ideas either.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Speak not to me of echoes, what...? I'm talking about Scylla's song. You've somehow learned to block it out, though... gods, I think I'm beginning to like it...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: How about I learn to block you out! With this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're very focused. Centered on the self. Become inured somehow to the incessant singing that always echoes through these halls.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Speak not to me of Echo's this or Echo's that, OK? Echo meant nothing to me and somehow got the wrong idea anyway. And don't you get any ideas either.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Echo meant nothing to you, what... I was talking about Scylla's song and how you've blocked it out. Come to think, I can't quite hear it now either.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: How about I learn to block you out! With this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, I can tell you're having trouble moving on. Maybe you ought to spend a little time down in the Fields. Clear your head, put the pieces of your heart back together, and all that?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you mean the Mourning Fields, I don't think that's a healing place. The Shades there wallow in self-pity and speechless despair. Although, there is one who repeats whatever I say to her...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Repeats whatever you say to her! I knew a Nymph like that one time. Nice girl. But she didn't get me! Only ever talked about herself. I had to cut her off.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So you do know Echo, the Mountain Nymph! She's languishing in the Fields even now. Apparently because of you. I can hardly get through to her...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, that's too bad. Well, tell her Narcissus really wants what's best for her. Unless that's me. Don't tell her that part, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I spoke to Echo about you. Said you didn't mean to hurt her, though perhaps I shouldn't have. She responded in her usual way but I could tell something happened. You broke her heart?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: She broke her own heart! They always fall for people who don't love them back. Anyway, I'm sure she'll find someone who thinks she's... awesome. Plenty of fish... in the sea!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, I've noticed. So, has anybody ever fallen out of love with you? Or do they all end up in the Mourning Fields, pining for you for eternity?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: All I know is no one's ever loved me as much as me. They're all too busy feeling sorry for themselves to really care, you know? Can't love someone... if you can't love yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey tell me something. Is Echo really not OK? I thought about the things you said, how she was all alone down in the Fields... how could I hurt someone without even trying, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Narcissus, don't take this the wrong way, but I'm impressed you've given any thought to someone else. As for Echo, she seems to be getting by. Is there something you'd like to say to her should I cross paths with her again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure, um... go ahead and tell her that I hope she finds someone who loves her like I love me. But don't tell her I said it, she'd just get the wrong idea and her heart would break... again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Do you think you'll ever visit Echo again in the Fields? You seemed to have no trouble getting there and back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, maybe I might! But it's a little crowded there, and I do like it here. So then again, maybe I won't! We made a clean break. Why mess with a perfectly good thing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know the strangest thing happened lately... I found myself thinking about Echo... as a friend. You said we're cursed but, I like mine. I'm blessed! But what about her?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Good question! Why don't you visit her again and ask yourself? You spoke to her of friendship. Friends share each other's burdens.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sort of like how you share the burden of this limitless supply of gifts, right? Here's the thing, though: I don't think I can leave this place again... now that the waters here are clear.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You'd be leaving only temporarily. And the love of your life is always with you, even if you can't always gaze longingly into his eyes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, all right, stop pushing me around! But if you do see Echo, tell her that her friend said hi, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I keep on thinking why is it so many friends can't just be friends? Like, what is wrong with that? They have to make it all romantic and then ruin everything!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps they're simply striving not to feel alone. We can't always control our feelings, or even tell what's causing them. So they get in the way of what's best for us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not for me! But probably for everybody else. Anyway, true friendship's just another type of love, and should be able to go through a rough patch every now and then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey wait, you're kind of like a friend of mine by now, aren't you? Unless you're playing the long game... make me let my guard down then go in for the kill! The ultimate suitor...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I understand your need to remain guarded when so many have attempted to win your affections even though you've made quite clear that you're not interested. But that's not me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't be too careful these days. So like, no sudden movements or anything, OK? If you're not here for me... must be for one of these.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey I wanted you to know, I've come to the conclusion that you really aren't here to sweep me off my feet. Instead, I've come to a conclusion that's... more disturbing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: More disturbing? Very well, I've braced myself. What's your conclusion about me, Narcissus? After all this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: My conclusion is you're only here for the free gifts I offer you repeatedly without a second thought. If I weren't so selfless, then no one would like me... for who I am.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Don't you think I'm capable of appreciating your company as well as your gifts? Your generosity is part of who you are. Though, why not put it to the test if you're uncertain?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...No! No, or... maybe. Look I'd not considered that before. The ramifications, augh! Please leave me to consider what this means. But first...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: What if I didn't give you any gift this time, what would you think about that, huh? I'm warning you, I'll do it!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Go right ahead, if it makes you feel better or lets you put your inhibitions to rest! I've little need for most resources that you offer at this point anyhow.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're bluffing! You want me to bestow one of my numerous offerings. Well I'll show you! I'm really gonna do it! This time... you get... nothing! How do you like that? Yeah!

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: It's quite all right. See you around!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...What are you doing here, Laurel? I'm not in search of suitors, I'm no longer giving anything away, and I know I'm not friendship material. Leave me be...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If your newfound concern is to become friendship material, you could start by getting my name right. I'm called Melinoë. Although, I'm getting used to Laurel more and more...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...And just for that, no gift from me this time either! Some friend I am, right? Now let me get back to giving my undivided attention to the only one I really care about.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You're a good friend, man. And much more. Always here for me, you know? Except that time the waters got all murky, but then Laurel, um... Melinoë... she really helped us out.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yet now you've taken to ignoring me because for some reason you find it difficult to fathom that I value your companionship, not just your numerous unwanted gifts.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not listening! And if my gifts are so unwanted, then I'm sure you wouldn't mind it if I didn't give another one to you again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Sometimes I think about how Echo's doing... how you're doing, meaning you not me... and then I start to wonder do you think about how I'm doing, too. It's messed up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I assure you it's perfectly fine. But you don't have to wonder what I think; you can just ask! Since I'll keep visiting from time to time, as long as that's all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll keep visiting even though I've ceased to offer you my gifts, so that your only reason to stop by is to see me?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Either you, or some Sea-Serpent, or some such.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, ever since I stopped giving you your choice of unwanted gifts, they really piled up... I've got some pretty good ones back there, too...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They're meant for you and no one else, Narcissus. I'm certain that your would-be suitors would prefer you keep them anyhow.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well funny thing about that is, some of these Shades, sounds like they've heard of you! But they are way too insecure to give you stuff directly, so... they hand it off to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What? So then some of those Verdure Samplers, Ancestral Offerings, and such... they were for me in the first place?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Probably most of them lately but I definitely lost count. So anyway, uh... here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, thanks for stopping by. I thought you'd definitely go away once I held out on giving you free stuff. Especially since a bunch of it was yours. But you didn't give up on me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You seemed as though you were just going through a phase, and not the sort an incantation could alleviate. It's true I didn't always think of you as a friend, Narcissus; but I do now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's funny, I... believe you. And since friends help each other out, why don't you take your pick of one of these. Those Shades back there brought plenty for the both of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: There was this wonderful aroma in the air just now... lilac on a summer breeze, or something like that. I thought it was you. But it's just the gift I gave you before. Good stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This Aromatic Phial you gave me does smell rather pleasant, doesn't it? I scarcely notice with all the brine and fishy odor around here. Though it doesn't seem to bother you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I just tune it out. Like all the rest. I give my undivided attention... to me... and I guess some of it to you, but you don't stay for long so it's OK.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I'm struck by how incredible I look, and am overcome by a trance-like affection that cannot easily be broken. Not by you, and not even by me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sounds familiar. How is what you're describing any different from what normally occurs when I see you, Narcissus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Don't worry, man, we can just tune everything out. Like the only thing that exists in this world is you and me. Just us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I have one rule, and that's look, but don't touch, OK? In fact, why don't you stand a little farther back, and also tell me how come you keep showing up like this?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've some affairs to settle in the lower reaches of the Underworld. Thought I'd take this rather scenic route, through this chamber of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it isn't my chamber. I just find the waters here particularly clean and reflective, and far fewer would-be suitors than in Asphodel. Remember to take a gift before you go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I wonder, what would it have been like were I not so unbelievably attractive? Some things would've been easier... not having everybody fall in love with me... maybe arithmetic...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're concerned that you're too winsome for your own good...? Well if it makes you feel any better, I don't find you particularly attractive...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I know you don't mean that. Though I appreciate you trying to comfort me. As a friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's hard to believe you're so completely in love with yourself. Don't you ever... consider the mistakes you've made, or ways in which you can improve? Or not improve, even...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: What, are you kidding? How could you possibly improve on this? Though, I definitely see your point. In your case.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Was that a bit of ridicule, or do you in fact have some keen bit of insight or feedback about me you're willing to share?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: If you're unhappy with the way you are, why don't you just, like, change? Or if you can't... get over it I guess, I don't know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know I was thinking... if you don't love me that must mean you've fallen pretty hard for someone else! So who's the lucky guy or gal or whatever?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That isn't any of your business... but, you've opened up to me. Look, I don't really fall in love the way you think. Tales of people falling all over themselves... I can't relate to any of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh that's OK! I can't relate to other people's love either. Anyway, as long as you've got something that keeps you going! And since you keep on showing up, I guess you do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I had plenty of suitors that said they wanted to be friends, but... they were still in love with me. I had to cut 'em off. How come it's harder to be friends than be in love, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps because... when it comes to feelings of infatuation, it takes effort to banish them, not to have them. But cultivating friendships... that takes work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I guess. I used to think I didn't need friends since I don't get lonely, but like... it's nice sometimes! Having somebody else to talk to about stuff. Like Echo, and like you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Huh, it's only you. Been getting crowded lately between all these chats and what's-her-name stomping around... least you're not her.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What's-her-name, that sounds like Nemesis. Scornful expression, dark hair and armor? What's your concern with her?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Gives me a bad vibe. Storms around, shakes everything up. Something about her, I don't know. Don't hurt my head, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Narcissus, how did you discover this place anyhow? I'd not expected to have a friendly chat along the outskirts of the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I always knew my way around the rivers and the seas. After I died, I got stuck in Asphodel for a while. Way too dry. So once the Underworld opened up, I swam straight here. Where it's cool.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey what's your problem, anyway? Always huffing through here like you're late to something. You got to learn to relax.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sounds like you were there when the Underworld opened up. It shouldn't have.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: How come? That way I'd still be shriveled up in Asphodel! Know what, though, just forget I asked. You're killing my mood here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Don't worry, man, she's not so bad, she doesn't stay for long. Soon it'll just be you and me again... and all those Shades back there.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Pardon the interruption, Narcissus. Were you just... speaking to your own reflection there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: They're always quick to judge, aren't they? Look, Laurel, or whatever your name is: You'll never understand what I've got. Don't even try.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...What would you say is your best quality, if you had to choose? The one thing you most adore... what drives everyone around you wild...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I, erm... well... I'm quite attentive, I think. If you tell me something, you don't have to tell me twice! Chances are I'll take it to heart the first time. Though, why do you ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Pff, there she goes, butting into our private conversations! I know your best quality, man: your looks. Whoever says looks aren't everything's a dummy. Like this girl over here! Just give her a gift, and she leaves us alone!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Got to say, your hair is looking awesome lately, you know? Whole wet-look thing! Must be all the humidity and brine. Keep it up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was about to thank you for the compliment when it occurred to me that you're just talking to yourself again, most likely. In which case, forgive the interruption!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry about it. I have plenty of time to myself. But you only get to see me, what, maybe once a day, tops? Better than nothing, though not nearly enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Hey remember the time you were, like, checking yourself out, and then a bee or something lands right on your face, so you swat it, bam! Your eye was swollen shut for like a week. You looked terrible, man!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Pardon, Narcissus. That sounds like it must have been a harrowing experience. Was your love for yourself tested when the reflection staring back at you had a big puffy eyelid?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: No! I mean... not really. But this love is more than skin-deep. Even if I can't look at me... I can still talk to me... and even if I can't do that, I can always think about me, you know? And so can you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Ever been to one of the local shows around here? Scylla and the Sirens keep playing night after night, much as I'd hoped they'd move on to the next stage of their tour by now...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Only music that I care for is my voice. The rest can get so loud, it disturbs the surface of the water. And whatever disturbs the surface of the water... disturbs me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Don't you get lonely sitting here, staring into those waters? Especially in a place like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Lonely? I don't know the meaning of the word. I have the best company you could ever ask for: Me!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're still wallowing in misery trying to see your reflection in a muddy pool. Why not just relocate? The waters aren't like this everywhere.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'll never leave you, man. I got us into this, I'll find a way to get us out, OK? So don't you quit on me! We'll figure something out, just need to stay focused. No distractions!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cutting to the front of the line! Whoever you are, at least you've got more guts than all my other would-be suitors back there. Though you still don't have a chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: These Shades must have felt strongly for you in life if they've been lingering nearby. But it wasn't my intent to be out of turn, merely to thank you for your gift the other night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I know every opening line there is, and merely to thank you is one of the classics. Acting all demure! Well I know what you really want, and you're not getting it. Just this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not seen you too much lately. I figured you'd moved on. But then I figured, that's impossible. They always come back.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've had a few things to take care of on the surface recently, so I've been spending more evenings up top. Though I appreciate you noting my absence.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I mostly noted more unwanted gifts piling up! Get rid of one of these for me, would ya? Maybe someone you know could use it... way up there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh it's you again. Look, I... want you to leave me alone, OK? Because I'm in a state of grief. And the only one I want to see me that way... is me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: State of grief, wait. What's with the water here...? It's gone all murky or something. Oh, no! You can't see your reflection anymore...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't need to rub it in, dummy! Not like you can do something about it anyway. A lot of things turn ugly after a while. So please excuse me while I mourn for what I've lost...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Didn't see you come in. Because I can't really see anything in here anymore... I'm sure the water's gonna clear up, though! At least, I think I'm sure...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Something's amiss. Such contamination typically never lasts more than a night or two. Have you checked other locations? The waters elsewhere don't appear like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're just saying that to lure me away so you can take my spot! And since when are you an expert on water filtration? Nah, these types of problems always fix themselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, I have done a lot of thinking, and... I've started thinking maybe that these waters aren't gonna clear up after all. I even checked a different spot, and sure enough... same thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Forget the waters for a moment, something's going on with you. Though whether you've done this to yourself or someone else has, I can't say. Have you been clinging to feelings of guilt, resentment, that sort of thing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: No way! I have never been better! Except when the waters were clear. I have never experienced feelings of guilt or resentment in my life. Unless you mean... well... huh.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: These waters are still filthy. I know all sorts of purification rites. You've aided me so often, the least I can do is help sort this out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: And what exactly do you have in mind, a bunch of soap? I don't think that's gonna work. It's a big sea out there! Nah, I think... I think I'm just gonna have to move on.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That may be just the thing! You said before you wished to mourn for what you've lost. You might attempt that in the Fields below, where Echo now resides. If you can bury your past with her, perhaps your aura may be cleansed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You think me talking to Echo's gonna clear things up? Well... couldn't hurt, I guess. And they love me down in the Fields! Tell you what. I'm gonna... give it some thought...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hey. Yeah I'm still here and everything. Though I've been thinking of a visit to the Fields one of these days... nights? I dunno.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Take all the time you need. I know it must be difficult, and the trip is treacherous. You could accompany me if you wish?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sounds like a date, no thanks. I get around just fine all by myself. But for now... I can still see a bit of me in here if I squint hard enough. It's a good look...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I talked to Echo like you said I should. What gives? This place is still the same! I thought you were gonna fix everything.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I didn't make myself clear. You have to fix everything, not me. You and Echo each are cursed, and your curses now are tangled up. You have more to discuss and sort out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you mean I'm cursed, you're cursed, I mean I've got it all! You don't know what you're talking about...! Unless you do...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I talked to her again just like you said, but nothing's changed in here. Though I feel kind of different anyway. I guess I'll have to settle for that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fear not, I keep my vows; I still have a purification rite to complete. I appreciate that you had the courage to meet with Echo, even if it was for your own sake.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah, well, that's love for you, right? I guess I'll just... hang out here till you do your thing. The Fields are nice and all, but... too many would-be suitors over there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Laurel, look! The waters are all clear! You don't know how glad I am to see me. You must have done your thing! I'd give you a hug or something, but... I wouldn't want you to take it the wrong way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Excellent. So then the purification worked out after all. Echo must have taken whatever you said to heart. Perhaps you're not as hopelessly self-absorbed as I feared.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Say what? Sorry, I was just... yeah, I mean, just look at this, the chin, the turn of the cheek... have you ever seen anything like it? Tell Echo I said hi. But as a friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You can't have my looks, but you can have this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't mean to be rude, but I'm kind of in the middle of something here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Tread softly, OK? I like it when the waters here are perfectly still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I wonder... how could a face be any better looking than this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Some come here to reflect... but nobody reflects better than me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: For somebody who's not a would-be suitor, you sure come around a lot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Those eyes... that jawline... the hair... oh man, the hair...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Wow, you know you really do look great tonight, man...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry, I'm sure you'll find someone who loves you at some point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's you again. Just help yourself I guess, and leave the rest to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: No one's going to help you if you don't help yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got everything I need right here. Plus all this stuff I welcome you to take!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm already spoken for, but all this other stuff is up for grabs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Good to see your face again, man! Don't even worry about her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Look at yourself, man! You're too good for any of these dummies.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Take one of these, maybe it'll help you get your own suitors.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I felt that piercing gaze upon me once again and figured it was you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: These waters are so clear, they really bring out the best in me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're always looking out for me! And you found me. So good job.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Congratulations finding me again! Now take your prize, and go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Stay as long as you like! Or better yet, pick one of these and go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Take what you want, but only one. Not playing favorites here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why don't you take one of these away from me, then go away yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Get your own reflective pool and choice of complimentary gift!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ungh, still not a single dent in my stockpile of stuff to give away...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh hey! Uh, since you're not a would-be suitor, feel free to cut in line.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: What's new, Laurel, same old same old? OK good seeing you, buh-bye!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you're still kind of obsessed with me, but I get it, believe me I do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Look at the definition in the chin... the hue of the eyes... the curl of the lip...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...When our eyes meet like this, nothing else matters... and they meet like this a lot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Stare for as long as you require, then take one of these.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: My love is unavailable, but I have lots of other gifts to give away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Just thinking about how great I am, you know? It happens all the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're looking at the one-and-only... and I should be looking at me now, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I wasn't born this attractive... oh who am I kidding, yes I was.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not seen you back here in a while... not that I'd notice either way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You just keep showing up, huh... well I would definitely too if I were you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: There's a lot to like about this place... and you are looking at it right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I try to blink as infrequently as possible, so as not to lose sight of what counts...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Hey beautiful... looks like you've got a visitor... give her a gift already, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, would you look at that. Another would-be suitor journeyed far and wide, just to gaze upon me, not that I blame you... and get totally rejected! Fine. Ogle if you must.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You have the wrong idea. I'm not seeking a mate. I don't even know who you are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: What? I'm Narcissus, dummy! The Narcissus? And if you're no would-be suitor... then why don't you relieve me of one of these innumerable gifts the others offered in vain. Then beat it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: As you've made no attempt to snap my head from my shoulders unlike many other things lurking about, I'm compelled to give you this. Just as a casual acquaintance, to be clear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'd give me such tokens of affection, full knowing I've already got a lot of them and, more importantly, am spoken for? Fine! Here's something to remember me by.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, a present, you're so thoughtful and all that. Don't mean to be rude about it... just don't get the wrong idea, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If I'm to keep taking from your copious supply of unused gifts, seems only fair that I give something in return from time to time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! How thoughtful, and all that. You dummy, though! Don't you realize I'm just going to add this to the pile with the rest, so that some other would-be suitor ends up taking it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sure, it's a gift! You get to decide what to do with it. Perhaps one of those other would-be suitors would enjoy it, if that's what you want.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh whoa, for me, how thoughtful. Look: Try all you like, I can't requite your love. Thought I'd make myself about as clear as these waters, just in case you've got the wrong idea again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not every gesture needs to be interpreted as having some romantic overtones, does it? You've made your feelings unambiguous, and I appreciate that. As a friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, some Nectar, how thoughtful! I used to mean it when I said it, but... there comes a point you get so many gifts you don't know what to say anymore. But you stick to how thoughtful anyway...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: In my family, the act of giving is a social custom, and a simple show of courtesy if nothing else. It should be thoughtful, as you say. But it can also be reflexive I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: You really want to hear me say it again, huh? All right fine! How thoughtful! How. Thoughtful!! That's my line! But you know what? I'm starting to think you might not be so thoughtful after all!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Narcissus, please, it's not my wish to offend. You don't have to take the Nectar if you don't want it. I just noticed you don't seem to have much of it around! Have you even tried it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you mean, tried it? Tried it how? All I've tried to do, Laurel, is look into these longing eyes in peace. Though I'll accept your Nectar, just this once, for the last time, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know you said that you're not interested in Nectar anymore, but I did want to give you this. To our continued friendship, and for all you've done for me these many nights!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: This is Ambrosia, whoa... this stuff is for the gods, not river spirits, no matter how uncommonly attractive they may be! What's the catch?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The only catch is that you have to stop misreading my intentions whenever I occasionally give you things. Far less often than you give me things, I might add!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well I don't know... but having given it some extra thought, I will accept. But just this once, OK? Maybe I'll share this... with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know how to thank you for the water-cleaning thing. So crisp and clear! I can see every perfect feature on this face. You've got a real future in water sanitation!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps the improved image quality will help you start to notice details other than yourself. The effect of the purification should last longer if you do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: What I'm trying to say is, I've decided to give to you the greatest gift of all that I've received. So here's something from my private reserve!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Uh whoa what's wrong with you? Coming in here like that. You don't need me, dummy. You need a doctor, or healer, or something.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, well, I've found those somewhat difficult to locate around here. Though on the flip side, no shortage of fishlike monstrosities determined to cut me a set of gills.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah that's what they all say. Look, there's probably a fountain or something somewhere around here. Wash up, come back, we'll talk.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew, you are never gonna find a mate looking all scratched up like that, bleeding everywhere. Might have something you could use to patch yourself up, if it'll make you go away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I still can't see myself. Am I still here? Are any of us here? Man...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ugh, why did this have to happen to me? To us? It isn't fair!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Please leave me to my sorrow. But take a complimentary gift on your way out!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Man... this is the worst thing that's ever happened to anyone...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Still can't see anything in here... but if I squint, or tilt my head a certain way...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm kind of in a crisis here, so just help yourself, and go.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Are you all right? Nemesis can be rather terse. She didn't hurt you or anything, did she?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: She said something just there... about how I'm stuck forever in the company of a buffoon. She couldn't have meant... no way. She's jealous. She likes me! And playing hard-to-get. Unlike some people, who come on a little strong.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't think Nemesis was playing hard-to-get. She wants to make certain you don't have too much of a good thing! Fortunately, I think she may leave you alone from here out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Is that so? Or are you just trying to get her out of the picture so you can have me to yourself? Expecting special favors just because you helped me with the water situation, eugh!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What? I don't want any special favors from you! Although... I fear I've grown accustomed to the gifts you've offered me each time I visit. How easily we take to certain habits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Narcissus_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah well, I've taken to getting a headache whenever you talk about this stuff. I'm gonna keep giving you these because I pity you... and because I don't want 'em.
